title: BEM-917 worker review
objective:
Create file governance/audit/internal/results/BEM917_WORKER_TELEGRAM_GD_REPORTS_REVIEW.md with content # BEM-917 WORKER TELEGRAM GD REPORTS REVIEW
Status: WORKER_REVIEW_READY
Decision: APPROVED_WITH_NOTES
Findings:
- Operator remarks are valid and must be integrated into the unified BEM-916 protocol.
- Telegram must be bound to the General Director object by default: OBJ-GD-001 through EL-CUR-GD-001.
- This fixes the ambiguity of 'which curator' receives Telegram interaction.
- The General Director becomes the operator-facing entry and delivery point. It does not mean GD performs all tasks; GD Curator classifies and routes through Director/Worker according to policy gate.
- Report periodicity must be operator-configurable. Default value is 1 hour.
- Actual automated schedule behavior remains an operator gate if it changes repository workflow triggers; configuration state can be prepared autonomously.
- Telegram reports must follow the same final-report canon used by the system: BEM, date/time, stage progress, roadmap progress, checklist with proof, table with next action and rationale.
Autonomous scope:
- Update protocol wording, schemas, command grammar, report templates, report interval state, deterministic parser/selftest plan.
Operator gate:
- Real Telegram token/secrets, production webhook mutation, schedule trigger activation, always-on daemon, issue #31 writes.
Recommendation:
- Approve BEM-917 update with notes.
- Status: UNIFIED_PROTOCOL_APPROVED_WITH_GD_TELEGRAM_AND_CANON_REPORTS.
No issue comments.
